news_items.headline: Apple beats on Q2 earnings, thanks to China & iPhone sales
news_items.summary: Apple (AAPL) reported second quarter results on Thursday after the closing bell. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $2.01 (compared to analyst estimates of $1.96), and revenue came in at $111.2 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $109.66 billion). Yahoo Finance's Josh Lipton takes a closer look at the breaking numbers.

news_items.summary: Apple (AAPL) will be reporting fiscal second quarter earnings results after Thursday's market close; this will be the company's first earnings release since CEO Tim Cook announced his plans to step down in September and hand the leadership reins off to John Ternus. Julie Hyman breaks down Wall Street's expectations for the iPhone maker.

news_items.headline: What Apple's new CEO announcement signals ahead of earnings?
news_items.summary: Wall Street investors have been abuzz around Apple (AAPL) this past week after CEO Tim Cook announced his plans to depart from the chief executive role this September, handing the reins off to John Ternus. Apple is also expected to report its latest earnings results this Thursday, April 30. Baird senior research analyst of cloud software William Power comes on Market Domination to discuss these headlines making waves ahead of Apple's earnings release.

news_items.headline: Apple Sales Top $111 Billion in Second Quarter, Powered by iPhone 17
news_items.summary: The company, which reported strong sales in China for a second quarter in a row, said iPhone revenue rose nearly 22%.

news_items.headline: Apple sales beat expectations powered by Mac, but iPhone hits supply constraints
news_items.summary: Apple on Thursday reported results that beat Wall Street estimates, with customers showing eagerness to buy a new MacBook model driven by incoming CEO John Ternus, ‌while supply constraints hindered iPhone sales. Apple said sales and profits were $111.18 billion and $2.01 per share for the fiscal second ‌quarter ended March 28, above analyst expectations of $109.66 billion and $1.95 per share, according to LSEG. Sales of the iPhone, still the company's best-selling product nearly 20 years after ​its introduction, were $56.99 billion, slightly less than estimates of $57.21 billion, according to LSEG data, after the biggest revamp of the lineup since the iPhone X in 2017.
